summary_sections.title: Lesson Overview
summary_sections.content_html:
Overview

This lesson covers the fundamental measures of center used to analyze and summarize data sets. You'll learn how to calculate and apply the mean, median, and mode, and understand when each is most appropriate.

summary_sections.title: Definitions and Concepts
summary_sections.content_html:
Key Definitions

What is the definition of 'Least-squares regression line (LSRL)'?

"Least-squares regression line (LSRL)" means: The line ŷ = a + bx that minimizes the sum of squared residuals; passes through the point (x̄, ȳ). The other options describe different concepts.

What is the definition of 'Residual'?

"Residual" means: The difference between observed and predicted values: residual = y - ŷ; positive means the model underpredicted. The other options describe different concepts.

What is the definition of 'Coefficient of determination (r²)'?

"Coefficient of determination (r²)" means: The proportion of variation in y explained by the linear relationship with x; ranges from 0 to 1. The other options describe different concepts.

What is the definition of 'Correlation coefficient (r)'?

"Correlation coefficient (r)" means: A measure of the strength and direction of the linear relationship between two quantitative variables; ranges from -1 to +1. The other options describe different concepts.

What is the definition of 'Residual plot'?

"Residual plot" means: A scatterplot of residuals versus the explanatory variable; used to check whether a linear model is appropriate for the data. The other options describe different concepts.
